content:
FINAL OWNER-CONTROLLED EAS, ANDROID, IOS, AND STORE RELEASE

Rork dependency removal is complete at the code, runtime, AI, GitHub,
Render, environment, and deployment levels.

Complete the remaining owner-controlled mobile build and release setup.

Do not restore any Rork dependency.
Do not create the EAS project under a Rork account.
Do not use Rork signing credentials.
Do not change the Android package name or iOS bundle identifier.

==================================================
1. INITIALIZE OWNER EAS PROJECT
==================================================

Use the IVX owner Expo/EAS account.

From the production repository:

cd expo
bun x eas login
bun x eas init

Requirements:

- EAS owner must be the IVX owner account.
- Create a new owner-controlled EAS project.
- Preserve the current Android package name.
- Preserve the current iOS bundle identifier.
- Write the generated EAS project ID into app.json or app.config.
- Do not add any Rork project ID.
- Do not add any Rork plugin or environment variable.
- Commit the configuration to the owner GitHub repository.

Return:

EAS_OWNER=
EAS_PROJECT_ID=
ANDROID_PACKAGE=
IOS_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=
CONFIG_FILE_CHANGED=
GITHUB_COMMIT_SHA=

If interactive owner login is required, stop only at the login screen and
return the exact action the owner must approve. Continue automatically after
authentication.

==================================================
2. AUDIT AND CREATE EAS BUILD PROFILES
==================================================

Verify eas.json contains owner-controlled profiles:

development
preview
production

Production requirements:

- Android build type: app-bundle
- Android signing: owner-controlled
- iOS distribution: store
- production API URL
- production Supabase configuration
- no localhost
- no exp://
- no Metro dependency
- no Rork variables
- no Rork runtime
- no Rork build service dependency

Verify:

EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=https://api.ivxholding.com

Do not expose backend secrets in the mobile application.

==================================================
3. ANDROID SIGNING OWNERSHIP
==================================================

Create or import an owner-controlled Android signing key.

Requirements:

- preserve package name;
- preserve versionCode progression;
- preserve versionName;
- store signing credentials under the owner EAS account;
- record the certificate SHA-1;
- record the certificate SHA-256;
- never expose the private keystore or passwords in reports;
- confirm Rork has no signing access.

Return:

ANDROID_PACKAGE=
VERSION_NAME=
VERSION_CODE=
SIGNING_OWNER=
CERTIFICATE_SHA1=
CERTIFICATE_SHA256=
RORK_SIGNING_ACCESS=false

==================================================
4. BUILD OWNER-CONTROLLED ANDROID ARTIFACTS
==================================================

Build:

1. Preview/testing APK
2. Production signed AAB

Commands:

bun x eas build --profile preview --platform android
bun x eas build --profile production --platform android

Verify:

- clean build succeeds;
- no Rork authentication requested;
- no Rork package downloaded;
- no Expo Go required;
- no Metro required;
- app launches from Android launcher;
- production API is used;
- owner login works;
- Admin HQ works;
- IVX Owner AI works;
- members load;
- investors load;
- CRM loads;
- variables vault loads;
- media upload works;
- session persists after restart.

Return:

APK_BUILD_ID=
APK_ARTIFACT=
APK_SHA256=
AAB_BUILD_ID=
AAB_ARTIFACT=
AAB_SHA256=
BUILD_COMMIT_SHA=
CLEAN_INSTALL_RESULT=
OWNER_LOGIN_RESULT=
AI_CHAT_RESULT=

==================================================
5. CLEAN ANDROID DEVICE ACCEPTANCE TEST
==================================================

Install the preview/release APK on a clean Android device.

Required test:

1. Uninstall old IVX development builds.
2. Confirm Expo Go is not used.
3. Install the generated APK.
4. Launch from Android launcher.
5. Confirm package name.
6. Confirm version.
7. Confirm build SHA.
8. Sign in as owner.
9. Open Admin HQ.
10. Open Members.
11. Open Investors.
12. Open Buyers.
13. Open JV Deals.
14. Open Influencers.
15. Open Realtors.
16. Open Brokers.
17. Open Tokenized Investors.
18. Open IVX Staff.
19. Open CRM.
20. Open Properties.
21. Open Transactions.
22. Open Variables.
23. Open Developer Workspace.
24. Send a real IVX AI message.
25. Receive a real response.
26. Restart the app.
27. Confirm owner session persists.
28. Confirm chat history persists.
29. Upload an image.
30. Upload a PDF.
31. Test notifications.
32. Confirm no Rork service is contacted.

Provide continuous screen recording and sanitized device logs.

==================================================
6. GOOGLE PLAY OWNER CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Use the owner Google Play Console account.

Required owner actions only when necessary:

- create or verify the IVX app in Play Console;
- create a Google Play service account;
- grant only required release permissions;
- provide the service-account JSON through the secure owner vault or approved
  local path;
- never paste the JSON into chat;
- never commit it to GitHub.

After owner configuration:

1. Connect EAS Submit to Google Play.
2. Upload the signed AAB to Internal Testing.
3. Verify package name.
4. Verify versionCode.
5. Verify signing certificate.
6. Verify store listing ownership.
7. Verify internal tester installation.

Return:

PLAY_CONSOLE_OWNER=
APPLICATION_ID=
SERVICE_ACCOUNT_VALIDATED=
INTERNAL_TEST_RELEASE=
RELEASE_VERSION_CODE=
RELEASE_STATUS=

==================================================
7. IOS OWNER CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Use the owner Apple Developer and App Store Connect accounts.

Required owner-controlled items:

- Apple Developer Team
- App Store Connect application
- distribution certificate
- provisioning profile
- App Store Connect API key
- issuer ID
- key ID
- private .p8 file stored securely
- push notification credentials
- associated domains where applicable

Never expose the .p8 contents.
Never commit Apple credentials to GitHub.

Build:

bun x eas build --profile production --platform ios

Then submit:

bun x eas submit --profile production --platform ios

Return:

APPLE_TEAM=
IOS_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=
IOS_BUILD_ID=
IOS_ARTIFACT=
IOS_BUILD_NUMBER=
APP_STORE_CONNECT_APP=
TESTFLIGHT_STATUS=
RORK_APPLE_ACCESS=false

==================================================
8. VERIFY SOURCE AND BUILD SHA MATCH
==================================================

The final values must match:

OWNER_GITHUB_HEAD_SHA
=
RENDER_DEPLOYED_SHA
=
BACKEND_COMMIT_MARKER
=
ANDROID_BUILD_SHA
=
IOS_BUILD_SHA
=
WEB_BUILD_SHA

If the mobile build cannot embed the complete SHA, embed and display an
immutable build marker mapped to the exact source commit.

Return:

GITHUB_SHA=
RENDER_SHA=
BACKEND_MARKER=
ANDROID_SHA=
IOS_SHA=
WEB_SHA=
MATCH=true/false

Repair any mismatch before release.

==================================================
9. FINAL RORK-INDEPENDENCE SCAN
==================================================

Scan source, lockfiles, build artifacts, APK/AAB metadata, environment
configuration, network traffic, Git remotes, webhooks, and runtime logs for:

rork
@rork
withRorkMetro
RORK_
EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK
rork.com
Rork AI Cloud
Rork proxy
Rork deploy router
Rork Git router

Classify historical documentation separately.

Required production result:

RORK_PACKAGES=0
RORK_RUNTIME_REFERENCES=0
RORK_BUILD_DEPENDENCIES=0
RORK_AI_DEPENDENCIES=0
RORK_DEPLOY_DEPENDENCIES=0
RORK_ENVIRONMENT_DEPENDENCIES=0
RORK_NETWORK_CALLS=0
RORK_SIGNING_ACCESS=0

==================================================
10. FINAL EVIDENCE PACKAGE
==================================================

OWNERSHIP

- GitHub owner
- Render owner
- Supabase owner
- EAS owner
- Android signing owner
- Google Play owner
- Apple Developer owner
- App Store Connect owner
- AI provider owner

BUILD

- Android APK
- Android AAB
- iOS build
- package name
- bundle identifier
- versionName
- versionCode/build number
- artifact checksums
- signing fingerprints

DEPLOYMENT

- GitHub SHA
- Render deploy ID
- Render SHA
- health HTTP 200
- web SHA
- Android SHA
- iOS SHA

LIVE TESTS

- owner login
- session restart
- Admin HQ
- access control
- members
- investors
- CRM
- properties
- transactions
- variables
- Developer Workspace
- IVX Owner AI
- chat persistence
- media upload
- notifications

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

RORK DEPENDENCY REMOVAL AND OWNER MOBILE RELEASE VERIFIED

or

OWNER MOBILE RELEASE INCOMPLETE

Do not return COMPLETE unless:

- EAS belongs to the owner;
- signed APK exists;
- signed AAB exists;
- APK passes clean-device testing;
- Google Play internal release exists;
- iOS build is produced or the only remaining step is an explicit Apple owner
  account approval;
- no Rork production dependency remains;
- no Rork signing access remains;
- all source/build SHAs are reconciled;
- evidence is complete.

If owner authentication is required, return only:

OWNER ACTION REQUIRED

PLATFORM:
SCREEN OR URL:
ACTION:
WHY REQUIRED:
WHAT RORK WILL DO IMMEDIATELY AFTER:
